publications.title: Complimentary electrostatics dominate T-cell receptor binding to a psoriasis-associated peptide antigen presented by human leukocyte antigen C∗06:02.
publications.authors: Anand S, Littler DR, Mobbs JI, Braun A, Baker DG, Tennant L, Purcell AW, Vivian JP, Rossjohn J.
publications.executive_summary: A crystal structure of the psoriatic T-cell receptor clone Vα3S1/Vβ13S1 bound to HLA-C∗06:02 presenting the melanocyte autoantigen peptide VRSRRCLRL (derived from ADAMTSL5) reveals that complementary electrostatics drive recognition, with negatively charged TCR residues forming an extensive charge network with arginine-rich positions in both the peptide and the HLA α1 helix. This charged interaction interface maps to the polymorphic C1/C2 region of HLA-C∗06:02, the allele conferring highest genetic risk for psoriasis, and suggests that this HLA variant is particularly suited to present arginine-rich epitopes to acidic TCRs. The findings provide molecular insight into how autoreactive T cells target melanocyte antigens in psoriasis and expand understanding of TCR-HLA-C engagement mechanisms more broadly.
